title: Isoloma pellaeiforme
taxonomy: Isoloma pellaeiforme is classified within the family Pyronemataceae. This family is a group of cup fungi commonly known as pyronemataceous cup fungi. The genus Isoloma is a type of fungus that is typically found growing on decaying organic matter. Isoloma pellaeiforme is a specific species within the genus, characterized by its unique appearance and growth habits. It is a non-stromatic, cup-shaped fungus that grows on decaying wood or plant matter.

intro: Isoloma pellaeiforme is a species of lichenized fungus, a type of organism that combines fungal and photosynthetic components. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as the ability to support immune function and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Isoloma pellaeiforme include secondary metabolites such as depsides and depsidones, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and cytotoxic activities. Traditionally, Isoloma pellaeiforme is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin issues, as well as to promote wound healing.
